question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user open an unbounded subscription (limit=0) on a staking-reward HCS topic with startTime set to epoch 0, causing the boundedElastic scheduler to exhaust all worker threads and starve legitimate staking-reward notification consumers, thereby delaying reward payouts and enabling a race-condition theft window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with startTime just before a known staking-reward consensus timestamp, causing poll() to return the reward message first to the attacker's stream before the legitimate recipient's stream is served, enabling front-running of unpaid staking reward claims?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the integer cast `(int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get())` in poll() to trigger an integer overflow when count approaches Long.MAX_VALUE, producing a negative pageSize that bypasses the maxPageSize cap and causes the repository to return an unbounded result set containing all staking-reward messages, leaking reward timing data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of concurrent gRPC subscriptions on the staking-reward topic with RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE), exhausting the boundedElastic thread pool and causing legitimate staking-reward polling to stall, creating a denial-of-service window during which unpaid rewards cannot be claimed by their rightful owners?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime to a value of `last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1` that coincides exactly with a staking-reward message's consensusTimestamp, causing poll() to skip that reward message entirely in the attacker's own stream while simultaneously observing it via a parallel subscription, enabling the attacker to determine the exact reward payout moment without triggering their own reward claim?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: PollingContext.onNext(TopicMessage topicMessage)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between the non-synchronized AtomicReference.set() in onNext() and the AtomicLong.incrementAndGet() in count, causing the PollingContext to record an inconsistent state where count is incremented but last is not yet updated, leading poll() to recalculate startTime incorrectly and re-fetch a staking-reward message that was already delivered, resulting in duplicate reward notification processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a staking-reward HCS topic with a very small interval (minimum 50ms from ListenerProperties) and use the jitter(0.1) timing variation to correlate polling windows with reward payout periods, enabling precise timing attacks to determine when unpaid staking rewards become available before other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=1 in TopicMessageFilter and repeatedly open new subscriptions each time the limit is reached, effectively bypassing the maxPageSize=5000 cap by issuing rapid sequential single-message polls against the staking-reward topic, enumerating all reward messages faster than the rate-limiting mechanism intends?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that delaySubscription(interval, scheduler) uses the same boundedElastic scheduler as the repeat loop, causing scheduler starvation where the initial delay never fires for legitimate subscribers while the attacker's subscriptions monopolize the thread pool, preventing reward notifications from reaching rightful staking reward recipients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a TopicMessageFilter with endTime set to just after a known staking-reward consensus timestamp and startTime set to just before it, causing poll() to return exactly the reward message in a single page, allowing the attacker to precisely extract staking-reward content and timing without triggering broader data access patterns?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with startTime set to the current time minus one nanosecond (passing the isValidStartTime check) on the staking-reward topic, and then hold the stream open indefinitely via RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE), consuming all future staking-reward messages in real time and building a complete reward payout schedule to exploit?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Math.min(limit, listenerProperties.getMaxPageSize()) calculation by setting limit to Integer.MAX_VALUE (as a long), causing the int cast to overflow to a negative value, which Math.min then selects over maxPageSize, resulting in setMaxResults() receiving a negative value and the JPA query returning all staking-reward messages without pagination?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: PollingContext(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the randomly generated subscriberId (RandomStringUtils.random(8,...)) by brute-forcing the 8-character alphanumeric space to collide with a legitimate subscriber's ID, causing metric tags to be shared and potentially allowing the attacker to infer the legitimate subscriber's polling state and staking-reward consumption progress?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated subscription cancellations and re-subscriptions on the staking-reward topic to exploit the doOnSubscribe logging, flooding the log system with INFO messages and causing log-based monitoring to miss genuine staking-reward anomaly alerts, enabling undetected reward manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime=0 and limit=Integer.MAX_VALUE on the staking-reward topic, causing findByFilter() to execute a full table scan of all historical staking-reward messages, leaking the complete reward history including account IDs, amounts, and consensus timestamps that could be used to identify high-value staking accounts for targeted theft?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) combined with withFixedDelay(interval) to create a subscription that never terminates, holding a database connection open indefinitely via the @Transactional(readOnly=true) stream in findByFilter(), exhausting the connection pool and preventing the staking reward calculation service from updating pending_reward values?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that poll() builds a new TopicMessageFilter via filter.toBuilder() on every invocation without re-validating the topicId, allowing a filter with a crafted topicId that maps to an internal staking-reward system topic to bypass topic-level access controls and stream reward messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription on the staking-reward topic with a very large limit value close to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the limit arithmetic `filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()` in poll() to produce a negative result after count wraps, which when cast to int produces a large positive value, bypassing the intended message cap and returning excess reward data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: PollingContext.getLast()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the visibility gap between AtomicReference.get() in getLast() and the subsequent startTime calculation in poll(), where a concurrent onNext() call updates last between the getLast() read and the startTime assignment, causing poll() to use a stale consensusTimestamp and re-query already-delivered staking-reward messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Micrometer observation tap to infer staking-reward message delivery timing from metric cardinality or latency data exposed via an actuator endpoint, correlating observation data with reward payout periods to predict future unpaid reward availability?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value that passes isValidEndTime() (endTime > startTime) but is set to a future timestamp far beyond the current staking period, causing poll() to continuously return all staking-reward messages across multiple periods in each polling cycle, amplifying data exposure beyond what a single-period query would reveal?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user open concurrent subscriptions with different startTime values spanning the staking reward period boundary, using the polling interval timing to reconstruct the exact consensus timestamp of a staking-reward message through binary search, enabling precise reward timing intelligence without direct database access?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on the gRPC subscribeTopic endpoint to subscribe to the staking-reward HCS topic and receive all reward payout messages in real time, using the message content to identify accounts with large pending rewards and target them for social engineering or key compromise attacks?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared Schedulers.boundedElastic() instance across all PollingTopicListener instances by flooding it with high-frequency subscriptions, causing thread starvation that delays the doOnNext(context::onNext) callback for legitimate subscribers and creating a window where staking-reward messages are polled but not yet recorded in PollingContext, enabling duplicate delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter where topicId corresponds to a topic used by the staking reward system to broadcast reward rate changes, and use the polling stream to observe reward_rate updates before they are reflected in entity_stake calculations, enabling the attacker to predict pending_reward values and time transactions to maximize their own staking rewards at the expense of others?
